title: Information Criteria for Hyper-parameter Tuning
text: implement several information-criterion–based model selection procedures BRIERi.selection, including Bayesian information criterion (BIC), Mallows’ \(C_p\), generalized cross-validation (GCV), select tuning parameter \(\eta\) \(\lambda\). Bayesian Information Criterion (BIC). BIC defined \[\begin{equation} \label{eq:BIC} \mathrm{BIC}(\eta,\lambda) =   \frac{1}{n} \left\{   \mathrm{Dev}\!\left(y,\widehat{\mathbf{\beta}}_{\eta,\lambda}\right)   +     \log(n)\,\mathrm{df}(\eta,\lambda)   \right\}, \end{equation}\] \(\mathrm{Dev}(y,\widehat{\mathbf{\beta}}_{\eta,\lambda})\) denotes deviance evaluated fitted model tuning parameters \((\eta,\lambda)\), \(\mathrm{df}(\eta,\lambda)\) corresponding effective degrees freedom. Mallows’ \(C_p\). linear models known error variance \(\sigma^2\), Mallows’ \(C_p\) defined \[\begin{equation} \label{eq:Cp} C_p(\widehat{\mathbf{\mu}}) =   \frac{1}{n} \left\| \mathbf{y} - \widehat{\mathbf{\mu}} \right\|^2 +   \frac{2\sigma^2}{n}\, \mathrm{df}, \end{equation}\] \(\widehat{\mathbf{\mu}}\) denotes vector predicted means \(\mathrm{df}\) associated degrees freedom. Generalized Cross-Validation (GCV). Generalized cross-validation provides computationally efficient approximation leave-one-cross-validation given \[\begin{equation} \label{eq:GCV} \mathrm{GCV}(\lambda) =   \frac{\mathrm{Dev}(y,\widehat{\mathbf{\beta}}_{\lambda})} {n \left\{ 1 - \mathrm{df}(\lambda)/n \right\}^2}. \end{equation}\]

title: Information Criteria for Hyper-parameter Tuning
text: implement several model selection procedures BRIERs.selection summary-level data, including pseudo-validation, Bregman-divergence–based Mallows’ \(C_p\) (\(BRC_p\)), generalized information criteria (GIC), select tuning parameters \(\eta\) \(\lambda\). Pseudo-validation. Originally proposed LASSOsum , pseudo-validation selects tuning parameter \(\lambda\) maximizing objective \[\begin{equation} \label{eq:pseudoval} f(\lambda) =   \frac{\mathbf{\beta}_{\lambda}^{\top}\,\widehat{\mathbf{r}}} {\sqrt{\mathbf{\beta}_{\lambda}^{\top}\,\widetilde{\mathbf{X}}_{0}^{\top}   \widetilde{\mathbf{X}}_{0}\,\mathbf{\beta}_{\lambda}}}, \end{equation}\] \(\widetilde{\mathbf{X}}_{0}\) standardized genotype matrix external reference panel \(\widehat{\mathbf{r}}\) shrinkage-adjusted estimate SNP–phenotype correlation vector. Specifically, element \(\widehat{\mathbf{r}}\) defined \[   \widehat{r}_j   =     r_j\left(1-\mathrm{fdr}_j\right),   \] \(\mathrm{fdr}_j\) denotes local false discovery rate SNP \(j\). objective approximates Spearman correlation predicted genetic score \(\widetilde{\mathbf{X}}_{0}\mathbf{\beta}_{\lambda}\) unobserved true phenotype. Bregman-divergence based \(C_p\). summary-level settings, adopt Bregman-divergence based extension Mallows’ \(C_p\), referred BRC\(_p\). criterion defined \[\begin{equation} \label{eq:BRCp} \widehat{R}_{\mathrm{BRC}_p} \!\left(\widehat{\mathbf{\beta}}_{\eta,\lambda}\right) =   D_{\mathcal{E}_{\widetilde{\mathbf{\Sigma}}}} \!\left(   \widehat{\mathbf{\beta}}_{\eta,\lambda},   \widehat{\mathbf{\beta}}   \right) +   \frac{|\mathcal{S}_{\eta,\lambda}|}{n(1+\eta)} \, D_{\mathcal{E}_{\widetilde{\mathbf{\Sigma}}}} \!\left(   \widehat{\mathbf{\beta}}_{\eta,\lambda},   \widehat{\mathbf{\beta}}   \right), \end{equation}\] \(D_{\mathcal{E}_{\widetilde{\mathbf{\Sigma}}}}(\cdot,\cdot)\) denotes Bregman divergence induced quadratic loss respect linkage disequilibrium (LD) matrix \(\widetilde{\mathbf{\Sigma}}\), \(\widehat{\mathbf{\beta}}\) pilot reference coefficient estimate, \(|\mathcal{S}_{\eta,\lambda}|\) size active set associated tuning parameters \((\eta,\lambda)\). factor \(1+\eta\) accounts effective sample-size inflation induced external model integration. Generalized Information Criterion (GIC). implement generalized information criterion summary-level models, defined \[\begin{equation} \label{eq:GIC_s} \mathrm{GIC}(\eta,\lambda) =   \frac{1}{n} \left\{   G\!\left(\widehat{\mathbf{\beta}}_{\eta,\lambda}\right)   +     a_n\,\mathrm{df}(\eta,\lambda)   \right\}, \end{equation}\] \(G(\cdot)\) denotes goodness--fit measure expressed terms summary statistics, \(\mathrm{df}(\eta,\lambda)\) effective degrees freedom defined previous section, \(a_n\) penalty term controlling model complexity. Throughout work, set \(a_n = \log(n)\), corresponding Bayesian information criterion (BIC). BRIERs, goodness--fit term \(G(\cdot)\) evaluated using Bregman divergence associated quadratic loss induced linkage disequilibrium matrix.

title: Information Criteria for Hyper-parameter Tuning
text: Accurate estimation degrees freedom (DoF) essential information-criterion–based model selection procedures. BRIERi BRIERs can formulated penalized regression problems respect external-model–weighted response, denoted \(\widetilde{\mathbf{Y}}\) formulation, effective degrees freedom fitted model can explicitly expressed \[\begin{equation} \label{eq:df_weighted} \mathrm{df} =   \frac{\mathrm{df}(\widetilde{\mathbf{Y}})}{1+\eta}, \end{equation}\] \(\mathrm{df}(\widetilde{\mathbf{Y}})\) denotes degrees freedom associated weighted response, \(\eta\) integration weight. BRIER2, implement two approaches estimate degrees freedom penalized models. first approach adopts simple computationally efficient estimator uses number active predictors, .e., number nonzero regression coefficients fitted model, estimate degrees freedom. estimator shown consistent LASSO-type problems suitable regularity conditions . second approach implements general degrees--freedom estimator based generalized information criterion (GIC) . Specifically, degrees freedom defined \[\begin{equation} \label{eq:df_gic} \mathrm{df}_{\lambda} \;=\; \mathrm{tr} \left\{   \left[     \nabla^{2}_{\mathbf{\beta}}     Q_{\eta, \lambda}\!\left(\widehat{\mathbf{\beta}}_{\lambda}\right)     \right]^{-1}   \nabla^{2}_{\mathbf{\beta}}   \ell\!\left(\widehat{\mathbf{\beta}}_{\lambda}\right)   \right\}, \end{equation}\] \[   Q_{\eta, \lambda}(\mathbf{\beta})   =     Q_{\eta}(\mathbf{\beta})   +     p_{\lambda}(\mathbf{\beta})   \] denotes penalized objective function penalty function \(p_{\lambda}(\mathbf{\beta})\) \(\widehat{\mathbf{\beta}}_{\lambda}\) estimated coefficient vector tuning parameter \(\lambda\).

text: summary-level local data available, individual-level quantities \(\{\mathbf{X}, \mathbf{Y}\}\) accessed. Instead, assume availability summary-level proxy statistics \[ \mathbf{r} = \frac{1}{n}\mathbf{X}^{\top}\mathbf{Y}, \qquad \mathbf{R} = \frac{1}{n}\mathbf{X}^{\top}\mathbf{X}, \] \(\mathbf{r}\) denotes score vector \(\mathbf{R}\) denotes LD (correlation) matrix estimated local data. setting, BRIERs.multi extends ensemble framework BRIERi.multi estimating ensemble weights using least-squares proxy objective depends \(\mathbf{r}\) \(\mathbf{R}\). Let \[ \mathbf{B} = \left( \check{\mathbf{\beta}}_{1}, \ldots, \check{\mathbf{\beta}}_{K} \right) \] denote matrix collecting external coefficient vectors, let \(\mathbf{w}\) denote ensemble weights. Consider proxy quadratic loss \[ \frac{1}{n}\big\|\mathbf{Y} - \mathbf{X}\mathbf{B}\mathbf{w}\big\|_2^2. \] Expanding expression using \(\mathbf{r}\) \(\mathbf{R}\) yields \[ \frac{1}{n}\|\mathbf{Y} - \mathbf{X}\mathbf{B}\mathbf{w}\|_2^2 = \text{const} - 2\,\mathbf{w}^{\top}\mathbf{B}^{\top}\mathbf{r} + \mathbf{w}^{\top}\mathbf{B}^{\top}\mathbf{R}\mathbf{B}\mathbf{w}. \] Setting derivative zero gives closed-form estimator ensemble weights: \[ \widehat{\mathbf{w}} = \big(\mathbf{B}^{\top}\mathbf{R}\mathbf{B}\big)^{-1} \mathbf{B}^{\top}\mathbf{r}. \] summary-level composite external model obtained \[ \check{\mathbf{\beta}} = \sum_{k=1}^{K} \widehat{w}_k\,\check{\mathbf{\beta}}_{k}. \] aggregated coefficient vector can plugged directly BRIER.S objective, enabling information borrowing multiple external models without requiring individual-level local data independent validation set. BRIERs.multi therefore provides computationally efficient privacy-preserving approach multi-study integration summary-level constraints. code: library(BRIER2)  data("Data_BRIERs") data <- Data_BRIERs  ## Shared target-cohort preprocessing X <- data$target$train$X summary <- data$target$train$sumstats summary$corr <- p2cor(summary$pval, summary$n, sign = sign(summary$stats)) XtX.list <- calLD(X, summary)  X_val <- standardize_X(data$target$validation$X)[[1]] y_val <- standardize_X(as.matrix(data$target$validation$y))[[1]]  eta.list <- c(0, exp(seq(log(0.1), log(10), length.out = 10)))  ## Fit, tune, and visualize one external model at a time for (m in 1:3) {   fit <- BRIERs(     sumstats = summary,     XtX = XtX.list$XtX,     beta.external = data$beta.external[, m],     eta.list = eta.list   )   fit <- BRIERs.selection(     fit, criteria = "gaussian.mspe",     X.val = X_val, y.val = y_val   )   out <- plot.eta(     fit,     X = data$target$testing$X,     covar.data = data$target$testing$y,     criteria = "gaussian.mspe",     standardize.data = TRUE,     bootstrap = TRUE   )   print(out$plot) }

what: BRIER2: Bregman-Divergence-Based Regularized Integrative Estimator in Regression — BRIER2-package
title: BRIER2: Bregman-Divergence-Based Regularized Integrative Estimator in Regression — BRIER2-package
text: Penalized regression methods genetic risk prediction integrate pretrained external models external individual-level data target cohort data. Supports Gaussian, binomial, Poisson families LASSO, MCP, SCAD penalties. Includes individual-level (BRIERi), summary-statistics (BRIERs), full individual-level external data (BRIERfull) variants.

what: BRIER.FULL: integration of individual-level external data — BRIERfull
title: BRIER.FULL: integration of individual-level external data — BRIERfull
text: Fits penalized regression model integrates individual-level data one external cohorts target cohort. External observations assigned per-cohort weights (eta) tuned user-specified grid.

title: BRIER.FULL: integration of individual-level external data — BRIERfull
code: BRIERfull(   X,   y,   cohort,   family = c("gaussian", "binomial", "poisson"),   eta.list = c(0, exp(seq(log(0.1), log(10), length.out = 20))),   ...,   trace = FALSE,   ncores = max(1L, parallel::detectCores() - 1L),   parallel = (ncores > 1L) )

title: BRIER.FULL: integration of individual-level external data — BRIERfull
text: X numeric matrix predictors (n x p) combining target external cohort observations. y numeric response vector length ncombining target external cohort observations. cohort integer vector length n indicating cohort observation belongs . Use 0 target cohort 1, 2, ... external cohorts. family string specifying response distribution: "gaussian", "binomial", "poisson". eta.list list numeric vectors (one per external cohort) specifying eta grid cohort. single numeric vector replicated external cohorts warning. ... Additional arguments passed BRIERi.eta (e.g. penalty, alpha, nlambda, penalty.factor). trace Logical. TRUE, prints progress messages fitting. ncores Integer. Number cores parallel fitting. parallel Logical. TRUE non-Windows platform, fits eta grid parallel using parallel::mclapply.

title: BRIER.FULL: integration of individual-level external data — BRIERfull
text: object class "BRIER" containing: y response vector. y.external NULL (placeholder compatibility BRIERi). family response family. cohort cohort indicator vector. eta.list list per-cohort eta grids. eta.grid full combinatorial eta grid (matrix, rows combinations). res list BRIER.eta objects, one per eta combination. null.dev null deviance computed target cohort .

title: BRIER.FULL: integration of individual-level external data — BRIERfull
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ set.seed(1) n_target <- 200 n_ext <- 500 p <- 50 X <- matrix(rnorm((n_target + n_ext) * p), ncol = p) beta <- c(rep(1, 5), rep(0, p - 5)) y <- X %*% beta + rnorm(n_target + n_ext) cohort <- c(rep(0, n_target), rep(1, n_ext))  fit <- BRIERfull(   X, y, cohort, family = "gaussian",   eta.list = list(c(0, 0.5, 1, 2)),   penalty = "LASSO" ) } # }

what: Model selection for BRIER.FULL fits — BRIERfull.selection
title: Model selection for BRIER.FULL fits — BRIERfull.selection
text: Selects optimal eta combination lambda value fitted BRIERfull object using held-validation data. Information criteria (AIC, BIC, etc.) supported BRIERfull.

title: Model selection for BRIER.FULL fits — BRIERfull.selection
code: BRIERfull.selection(   object,   criteria = c("gaussian.mspe", "gaussian.rsq", "binomial.dev", "binomial.mcfrsq",     "binomial.tjursq", "binomial.auc", "poisson.dev"),   X.val = NULL,   y.val = NULL )

title: Model selection for BRIER.FULL fits — BRIERfull.selection
text: object object class "BRIER" BRIERfull. criteria string specifying validation criterion. One : "gaussian.mspe": mean squared prediction error. "gaussian.rsq": negative R-squared (minimized). "binomial.dev": binomial deviance. "binomial.mcfrsq": negative McFadden's R-squared (minimized). "binomial.tjursq": negative Tjur's R-squared (minimized). "binomial.auc": negative AUC (minimized). "poisson.dev": Poisson deviance. X.val numeric matrix validation predictors (n_val x p). y.val numeric response vector validation (length n_val).

title: Model selection for BRIER.FULL fits — BRIERfull.selection
text: object class c("BRIER.selection", "BRIER") extends input object following added elements: criteria selection criterion used. eta.min eta combination minimising criterion. eta.min.index corresponding row index eta.grid. lambda.min lambda value minimising criterion eta.min. lambda.min.index corresponding lambda index. eta.lambda data.frame summarising optimal lambda eta combination.

title: Model selection for BRIER.FULL fits — BRIERfull.selection
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ fit <- BRIERfull(   X, y, cohort, family = "gaussian",   eta.list = list(c(0, 0.5, 1, 2)),   penalty = "LASSO" ) fit_selection <- BRIERfull.selection(   fit, criteria = "gaussian.mspe",   X.val = X.val, y.val = y.val ) coef(fit_selection) } # }

title: Cross-validation for BRIER.I — BRIERi.cv

title: Cross-validation for BRIER.I — BRIERi.cv
code: BRIERi.cv(   X,   y,   family = c("gaussian", "binomial", "poisson"),   eta.list,   beta.external,   multi.method = c("ind", "PCA", "stacking"),   optim.args = list(),   ...,   nfolds = 5,   fold = NULL,   seed = NULL,   returnY = FALSE,   trace = FALSE,   ncores = max(1L, parallel::detectCores() - 1L),   parallel = (ncores > 1L) )

title: Cross-validation for BRIER.I — BRIERi.cv
text: X numeric matrix predictors (n x p). y numeric response vector length n. family string specifying response distribution: "gaussian", "binomial", "poisson". eta.list list numeric vectors (one per external model) specifying eta grid model. single numeric vector replicated external models warning. beta.external numeric matrix external model coefficients ((p+1) x M). Must include intercept first row. multi.method string specifying combine multiple external models: "ind", "PCA", "stacking". See BRIERi. optim.args list arguments passed optim() using stacking binomial Poisson families. ... Additional arguments passed BRIERi.eta. nfolds Integer. Number CV folds. fold Optional integer vector fold assignments (length n). provided, overrides nfolds seed. seed Optional integer seed reproducible fold assignment. returnY Logical. TRUE, per-fold predictions Y per-fold deviances E stored BRIER.eta object. trace Logical. TRUE, prints progress messages fitting. ncores Integer. Number cores parallel fitting. parallel Logical. TRUE non-Windows platform, fits eta grid parallel using parallel::mclapply.

title: Cross-validation for BRIER.I — BRIERi.cv
text: object class c("BRIER.cv", "BRIER") containing: y response vector. y.external matrix external predictions (n x M). eta.list list per-model eta grids. eta.grid full combinatorial eta grid. res list BRIER.eta objects CV fields attached (cve, cvse, lambda.min, lambda.min.index). null.dev null deviance. criteria Always "cve" CV. nfolds number folds used. fold fold assignment vector. eta.min eta combination minimising cve. eta.min.index corresponding row index eta.grid. lambda.min lambda value minimising cve eta.min. lambda.min.index corresponding lambda index. eta.lambda data.frame summarising optimal lambda per eta.

title: Cross-validation for BRIER.I — BRIERi.cv
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ set.seed(1) n <- 200 p <- 50 X <- matrix(rnorm(n * p), ncol = p) beta_true <- c(rep(1, 5), rep(0, p - 5)) y <- X %*% beta_true + rnorm(n) beta.external <- matrix(c(0, beta_true * 0.8), ncol = 1)  cv.fit <- BRIERi.cv(   X, y, family = "gaussian",   eta.list = list(c(0, 0.5, 1, 2)),   beta.external = beta.external,   nfolds = 5, seed = 42 ) coef(cv.fit) } # }

what: Core fitting function for BRIER.I — BRIERi.eta
title: Core fitting function for BRIER.I — BRIERi.eta
text: Internal function fits penalized regression model single eta combination vector. Called BRIERi, BRIERfull, BRIERi.cv. Performs response/predictor validation, standardization, lambda path setup, dispatches appropriate coordinate-descent C++ routine.

title: Core fitting function for BRIER.I — BRIERi.eta
code: BRIERi.eta(   X,   y,   weights = NULL,   eta = 0,   y.external = NULL,   family = c("gaussian", "binomial", "poisson"),   penalty.factor = rep(1, ncol(X)),   penalty = c("LASSO", "SCAD", "MCP"),   alpha = 1,   gamma = ifelse(penalty == "SCAD", 3.7, 3),   lambda,   nlambda = 100,   lambda.min = {      if (nrow(X) > ncol(X))           1e-04      else 0.05  },   log.lambda = TRUE,   max.iter = 1e+06,   eps = 1e-04,   nvar.max = NULL,   returnX = FALSE )

title: Core fitting function for BRIER.I — BRIERi.eta
text: X numeric matrix predictors (n x p). y numeric response vector length n. weights Optional numeric vector observation weights (length n). Defaults equal weights. eta numeric vector external-model weights (length M). Use eta = 0 ignore external information. y.external Optional numeric matrix external predictions (n x M). family string: "gaussian", "binomial", "poisson". penalty.factor numeric vector per-variable penalty multipliers (length p). Variables multiplier 0 unpenalised. penalty string specifying penalty: "LASSO", "MCP", "SCAD". alpha numeric scalar elastic net mixing parameter. gamma numeric scalar MCP SCAD penalties. lambda Optional user-specified lambda sequence. nlambda integer specifying number lambda values. lambda.min numeric scalar minimum lambda ratio. log.lambda Logical. TRUE, lambda sequence log-spaced. max.iter integer specifying maximum total iterations. eps numeric scalar convergence tolerance. nvar.max integer specifying maximum number selected variables. returnX Logical. TRUE, standardised data list returned output diagnostics.

title: BRIER.I: integration of pretrained external models with target individual-level data — BRIERi
text: Fits penalized regression model integrates one pretrained external prediction models target individual-level data. External predictions integrated target response via tunable eta parameter (one per external model), lambda selected regularisation path.

code: BRIERi(   X,   y,   family = c("gaussian", "binomial", "poisson"),   eta.list = c(0, exp(seq(log(0.1), log(10), length.out = 20))),   beta.external = rep(0, ncol(X) + 1),   multi.method = c("ind", "PCA", "stacking"),   optim.args = list(),   ...,   trace = FALSE,   ncores = max(1L, parallel::detectCores() - 1L),   parallel = (ncores > 1L) )

title: BRIER.I: integration of pretrained external models with target individual-level data — BRIERi
text: X numeric matrix predictors (n x p) target cohort. y numeric response vector length n. family string specifying response distribution: "gaussian", "binomial", "poisson". eta.list list numeric vectors (one per external model) specifying eta grid model. single numeric vector replicated external models warning. beta.external numeric matrix external model coefficients ((p+1) x M, M number external models). Must include intercept first row. multi.method string specifying combine multiple external models: "ind": keep external models independent (default). "PCA": aggregate using first principal component beta.external.
